mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because then policy easing is interpreted as liquidity support rather than distress.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if 20-day SPX returns are not higher after monthly declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE above a recent trend marks labor-market deterioration, which raises recession risk and reduces near-term equity earnings expectations. That shock should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days, especially when payroll growth is also slowing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is not decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: An upward surprise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending, which supports revenue growth expectations and boosts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is most visible when housing activity is not collapsing, because broad demand then looks sustainable rather than transitory.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive RSAFS increases are not followed by above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is neutral/negative when HOUST is also falling.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and utilization, which raises expected cyclical earnings and supports equity prices over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism fails when the production gain is occurring alongside higher inflation, because then margins are likely being squeezed rather than expanded.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not higher following sustained INDPRO improvement, or if the relationship turns negative in periods when CPIAUCSL is also accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below recent levels signals weakening construction demand and broader cyclical softness, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is stronger when PAYEMS is also soft, because then housing weakness is part of a wider demand slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not associated with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect persists even when PAYEMS is improving.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL inflation trend while FEDFUNDS remains very low implies real rates are becoming more negative and policy is still accommodative, which supports risk assets over the next 5 trading days. This should fail once inflation acceleration triggers a tightening response or when rates rise materially.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising at an annualized pace above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the specified low-rate inflation regime does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns above baseline, or if returns become negative once FEDFUNDS starts rising.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates weakening payroll formation, which reduces expected household income growth and depresses equity earnings expectations over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be especially strong when UNRATE is simultaneously rising, because the labor market then signals a broad downturn rather than a statistical pause.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 3 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if sustained PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad real demand expansion, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through stronger sales and earnings expectations. The mechanism fails when GDP grows but retail sales contract, because then growth is not broad-based enough to support equities.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not precede above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is weak when one of the two series is flat or falling.
